div.head: 概述
div.p: 46號窟是C區三窟中最小的一座，但窟高仍超過3米。本卷的概述部分已經介紹了46號窟的獨特之處：入口兩側的前壁以及人字披頂，在整個臥佛院都獨一無二； 此窟似乎是C區三窟中最早開鑿的一座；窟內刊刻了兩位僧人的名字和一條紀年為733年的早期題記；此窟內刊刻的佛經數目是臥佛院所有刻經窟中最多的。
46號窟內共刊刻了十部佛經。它們的布局見平面圖，。平面圖中的縮寫表示如下：

list.item: 經錄：眾經目錄
摩訶：摩訶般若波羅蜜
德王：大般涅槃經：高貴德王菩薩品
海藏：佛性海藏智慧解脫破心相經
金剛：金剛般若波羅蜜經
頂經：佛頂尊勝陀羅尼經
脩羅：佛說脩多羅般若波羅蜜經
彌陀：佛說阿彌陀經
頂咒：佛頂尊勝陀羅尼咒
陀咒：阿彌陀佛咒

figure.head: 46號窟平面圖；雷魯卡根據馬本漢和王平國田野繪圖製作
Ground plan of cave 46; drawing Lukas Ledderose after field map by MB and MS
46號窟刻文區概覽，自右往左依次為d壁、e壁、和f壁
Overview of the fields of script in cave 46 (from right to left), on walls d, e, and f

list.item: 南麓，C區最西端，高於現今步道約4米（；）。 距B區42號窟約17米。
46號窟和51號窟入口間的隔牆寬：172釐米。

div.head: 形制
div.p: 平面呈矩形，進深大於寬度，向內漸寬。窟內地面在入口處與前廊齊平，向內擡升。入口兩側各有一道窄前壁；人字披頂，頂脊呈東西向貫穿窟室中心，與入口平行。窟頂後披較陡直，其高度低於前披。

list.item: a壁：高294釐米，寬15－33釐米。
b壁：高258釐米，寬70釐米。
c壁：高311釐米，寬52釐米。
d壁：前部高311釐米，中心靠近頂脊處高335釐米，後部高301釐米，寬304釐米。
e壁：高301釐米，寬371釐米。
f壁：前部高298釐米，中心靠近頂脊處高335釐米，後部高301釐米，寬303釐米。
g壁：高298釐米，寬47釐米。
h壁：高259釐米，寬72釐米。
i壁：高288釐米，寬70釐米。
入口：門楣高254釐米，入口前部寬168釐米，後部寬198釐米。

div.head: 改造
div.p:
門楣外的頂部向上擴鑿至與C區三窟中另兩窟窟外前廊的廊頂齊平。窟內頂部可能也曾加高。 見上文
靠近後壁（即e壁）東端的地面上鑿有一108×60釐米的矩形內凹區。更多的改造，見各壁“保存狀況”部分。

div.head: a壁
div.p: 斜向鑿痕，部分打磨，未刊刻內容。

div.head: b壁
div.p: 距地面約26釐米以上的區域粗略打磨，未刊刻內容。
有一用於固定門檻、40×8釐米的縱向凹槽。地面上方92－100釐米處和208－216釐米處各有一鑿孔。門楣下方亦有一鑿孔，距與a壁相接轉交處6釐米。壁面有些許遊人刻劃。

div.head: c壁
div.p: 距地面約87－95釐米以上的區域打磨，未刊刻內容。

div.head: 布局

list.item: 打磨區始於距c壁8－12釐米、頂脊下方約18釐米處，結束於距e壁9－20釐米、地面上方19－26釐米處。
刻字區282（包括中心突起部分）×256釐米，始於距c壁14－15釐米、頂脊下方25釐米處，結束於距e壁36釐米、地面上方23－33釐米處；下緣近水平。

div.p: 頂脊下方的區域，從第47至第66行，有一塊12×42釐米、向上凸起的矩形延伸區。第67和第68行的刻文僅刻至距地面193釐米處。靜泰的《眾經目錄》在此處結束。 第69行的七個大字將壁面分為前、後兩段。第70至第75行間又有一塊9×13釐米的上凸區域。第76至第118行刻文的止緣再次與第1至第46行的刻文齊平。

div.head: 保存狀況
div.p: 良好。壁面平整，下部風化。地面上方約87釐米處有一橫向窄裂隙自c壁延伸至d前三行刻經。該裂隙衍生出很多小裂紋，貫穿整個壁面。 另一條裂隙位於地面上方約110－117釐米處，水平延伸約110釐米。地面上方191－195釐米處、第30至第32行刻經間有一個人工鑿出的圓孔。

row.cell: 大唐東京大敬愛寺一切經論目序釋靜泰撰；標識靜泰撰寫的《大唐東京大敬愛寺一切經論目序》的開始。
眾經目錄卷第一；標識靜泰《眾經目錄》第一卷的開始。
眾經目錄卷第一；標識靜泰《眾經目錄》第一卷的結束。
眾經目錄卷第二；標識靜泰《眾經目錄》第二卷的開始。
……［辯中］邊［論二］卷，本行末；標識《辯中邊論》；乃靜泰《眾經目錄》刊刻的最末數字。
“摩訶般若波羅蜜”（以大字刻出）。
大般涅槃經高貴德王菩薩品之二 十三；標識《大般涅槃經》第十三卷“高貴德王菩薩品”第二部分的開始。在《大正藏》中，此品為該經第二十二卷。
大般涅槃經卷第十三；標識《大般涅槃經》第十三卷的結束，即是“高貴德王菩薩品”第二部分的結束。

figure.head: 通向 C區的臺階；2010年拍攝
Staircase leading up to section C; photograph taken in 2010

div.head: 布局

list.item: 打磨區： 整壁
僅在東半部有一塊270×174釐米的縱長方形刻經區；刻文從左向右刊刻，縱行因此也從左向右計數。e壁西半部未鑿刻內容。

div.p: 刻經區始於距f壁14－19釐米、距窟頂13釐米處，結束於地面上方20－24釐米、距d壁177釐米處。下緣接近水平。
刻經區的上半部有十幾處手掌大小的空白區，雖然經文應連貫刊刻於此。 見《中國佛教石經·四川省》第一卷第14頁，圖12，以及第33頁的討論。

row.cell: 有縱格線，自77行後繼續延伸約45釐米

div.head: 保存狀況
div.p: 良好。壁面較平整，下半部略風化。在壁面中心高約154釐米處有兩個人工開鑿的孔洞，東邊的孔洞在第63至第65行間，未影響刻經；西邊的洞在刻經區外。地面上方237釐米處另有一人工鑿孔，影響第77行刻經。 在刻經區外、地面上方39－66釐米處還有一個45釐米寬的巨大天然孔洞。

row.cell: ［佛性海藏智慧解脫破心相經（卷上）。如是我聞，一時佛……］；未刊刻；標識《佛性海藏智慧解脫破心相經》第一卷的開始。標題中應有足夠的空間刊刻“卷上”二字。
［二月］十五日臨般涅槃時；《佛性海藏智慧解脫破心相經》第一卷刊刻的首幾字。
佛性海藏智慧解脫破心相［經］卷上；標識《佛性海藏智慧解脫破心相經》第一卷的結束。

list.item: 與對面的d壁不同，窟頂下方有一個明顯的三角形山牆，打磨面上緣較平直。 打磨面自頂脊下方32釐米處始，結束於地面上方22－36釐米處；兩側分別延伸至e壁和g壁。
刻經區幾呈正方形，253×228釐米；開始於距e壁約12－27釐米、頂脊下方37釐米處； 結束於距g壁54－56釐米、地面上方30－37釐米處。第1至第56行的上緣平直，其後則呈不規則的水平。下緣亦大致呈水平。此刻經區內刊刻了四部佛經：
第1－51行，《金剛般若波羅蜜經》
第51－71行，《佛頂尊勝陀羅尼經》
第71－84行，《佛說脩多羅般若波羅蜜經》
第84－98行，《佛說阿彌陀經》

row.cell: 前51行，與界格同步，之後無
前51行；約2.5×2.5釐米，之後僅有縱向界格

table.head: 舉例，第一部佛經（金剛），界格內
舉例，第二部佛經（頂經），第一行仍在之前的界格內

div.head: 保存狀況
div.p: 良好；地面上方50釐米、自該壁與e壁轉角處起有一道水平裂隙，影響了前5行刻經。地面上方59－68釐米處的窟壁中央有一連串天然裂縫，影響到第37至第68行； 另一串較短的裂縫位於地面以上82－85釐米處，影響了第51至第67行。壁上還有幾處人工鑿出的溝槽和孔洞。一道140×8釐米的縱向鑿溝，距e壁77釐米，影響了第19至第24行刻經；另一道147×8釐米的縱向鑿溝，距g壁50釐米，打破了第95至第98行刻經； 這道鑿溝繼續延伸至地面，向洞窟中部深入52釐米，其後以直角轉向窟口，又延伸了60釐米。這兩道鑿溝應是20世紀60年代此窟用來畜養動物時，安裝木柵欄或石條之用。另外兩個鑿洞分別位於地面上方193－196釐米（第36至第37行）處和192－195釐米（第58至第60行）處。

row.cell: 金剛般若波羅蜜經；標識《金剛般若波羅蜜經》的開始。
金剛般若波羅蜜經；標識《金剛般若波羅蜜經》的結束。
佛頂尊勝陁羅尼經；標識《佛頂尊勝陀羅尼經》的開始。
佛頂尊勝陁羅尼經；標識《佛頂尊勝陀羅尼經》的結束。
佛說脩多羅般若波羅蜜經；標識《佛說脩多羅般若波羅蜜經》的開始。
脩多羅般若波羅蜜經；標識《佛說脩多羅般若波羅蜜經》的結束。
佛說阿經；標識《佛說阿彌陀經》的開始。
［……］難佛說［此經］……；《佛說阿彌陀經》可辨識的最後幾字。
［……聞佛所說，歡喜信受，（作禮而去）］；已損；《佛說阿彌陀經》最後幾字；《大正藏》版中有“作禮而去”，但敦煌寫經中無此四字。
［佛說阿弥陁經］；不可見；根據此壁上其他刻經推測，為《佛說阿彌陀經》經名的重覆。

div.head: g壁
div.p: 打磨面延伸至地面上方約61－76釐米處。未刊刻內容。

list.item: 打磨面為189×72釐米的縱長方形。從門楣下方18釐米處開始，結束於地面上方42釐米處，寬度即為壁寬。
刻經面為157×72釐米含邊框的縱長方形。刻經區外的打磨面上有一些潦草的刻字。

row.cell: 雙邊線；兩側邊線間寬1.0釐米；下邊線間寬約2.0釐米

div.head: 保存狀況
div.p: 表面平整。左上部有一大塊壁面崩毀，刻文第一行首字母因而被破壞；損毀區域呈三角形，並於左側向下延伸至壁面中部。刻經區下方有一道40×8釐米的縱向凹槽，用於固定門檻。兩個周圍覆滿碎紋的矩形鑿孔， 一個位於地面上方165－173釐米處，打破了第7至第10行刻經；另一個位於地面上方109－118釐米處，破壞了第13至第17行刻經。門楣下方有一個鑿孔，與i壁相接轉角處相距14釐米。
超過百分之八十的界格原本未填刻字。如今所存刻字多為後代潦草為之。

row.cell: ［佛］頂尊勝陁羅尼咒；標識《佛頂尊勝陀羅尼咒》的開始。
［莎（長引）婆］訶五十二；標識《佛頂尊勝陀羅尼咒》的最後一句，即第52句。
大唐開元二十一［年□］月二十日；標識帶有開元二十一年紀年的題記；其中“二”字被加了一橫改作“三”。
［阿弥阤佛說咒］；標識《阿彌陀佛說咒》的開始。

div.head: Overview
div.p: This is the smallest of the three caves in section C, but it is still more than 3 m high. In the Introduction to the present volume, the special features of cave 46 have been laid out: with its side walls at the entrance and its pitched roof, its architecture is unique in the Grove; the cave seems to be the earliest of the three caves in section C; it is inscribed with the names of two monks and one early colophon of 733; and it can boast the largest number of texts in any cave at the Grove.
In total ten texts are engraved. Their distribution is shown in the ground plan in fig. . The abbreviations used in this plan signify the following:

list.item: 德王： 大般涅槃經：高貴德王菩薩品 “Bodhisattva High and Noble Virtuous King”

item.foreign: 海藏：佛性海藏智慧解脫破心相經
item.hi: Sutra on the Wisdom Stored in the Ocean of Buddha-nature

item.foreign: 金剛：金剛般若波羅蜜經
頂經：佛頂尊勝陀羅尼經
脩羅：佛說脩多羅般若波羅蜜經
item.hi: Perfection of Wisdom in the Sutras

item.foreign: 頂咒：佛頂尊勝陀羅尼咒

list.item: Southern slope, westernmost cave in section C, ca. 4 m above present walkway (fig. and fig. ). Distance from cave 42 in section B ca. 17 m.
Width of the wall between the openings of caves 46 and 51: 172 cm.
Direction: wall d 325°, wall f 318°.

div.head: Shape
div.p: A rectangular ground plan, deeper than it is wide, widens toward the rear. The floor starts at the level of the passageway and rises toward the rear. Short walls at both sides of the entrance; the ceiling is pitched, with a ridge running east-west across the centre of the cave parallel with the front opening. The rear section of the roof is steeper and ends at a lower point than the front section.

figure.head: 46號窟平面圖；雷魯卡根據馬本漢和王平國田野繪圖製作
Ground plan of cave 46; drawing Lukas Ledderose after field map by MB and MS

list.item: Wall a: height 294 cm, width 15–33 cm.
Wall b: height 258 cm, width 70 cm.
Wall c: height 311 cm, width 52 cm.
Wall d: height at the front 311 cm, at midpoint beneath the ridge 335 cm, at the rear 301 cm, width 304 cm.
Wall e: height 301 cm, width 371 cm.
Wall f: height at the front 298 cm, at midpoint beneath the ridge 335 cm, at the rear 301 cm, width 303 cm.
Wall g: height 298 cm, width 47 cm.
Wall h: height 259 cm, width 72 cm.
Wall i: height 288 cm, width 70 cm.
Entrance opening: height of lintel above the floor 254 cm, width of opening front side 168 cm, rear side 198 cm.

div.head: Changes
div.p: The ceiling outside the door lintel has been extended upward to the level of the ceiling above the passageway in front of the three caves in section C. The inner ceiling may also have been heightened.See above, . Near the eastern end of the rear wall e, a rectangular depression, 108 x 60 cm, has been cut out from the floor. For more changes see state of preservation for each wall.

div.head: Wall a
div.p: Striations, partly polished, no engraving.

div.head: Wall b
div.p: A roughly polished area extends down to ca. 26 cm above the floor, no engraving.
Vertical incision, 40 x 8 cm, for a threshold. Two holes, 92–100 cm and 208–216 cm above the floor. A hole directly under the lintel, 6 cm from the corner with wall a. Some graffiti.

div.head: Wall c
div.p: A polished area extends down to ca. 87–95 cm above the floor, no engraving.

list.item: A polished area begins 8–12 cm from wall c, ca. 18 cm below the ridge; it ends 19–26 cm above the floor level and 9–20 cm from the rear wall e.

A field of script, 282 cm (including the protrusion in the center) x 256 cm begins 14–15 cm from wall c, and 25 cm below the ridge; it ends 23–33 cm above the floor and 36 cm from wall e; the lower edge seems to be approximately horizontal.

div.p: In the area beneath the ridge, from column 47–66, a rectangular extension of the field, 12 x 42 cm, protrudes upward. In columns 67 and 68 the engravings end about 193 cm above the floor. Monk Jingtai’s Catalog stops here. Column 69 with its seven oversize characters divides the wall into a front and a rear part. From columns 70–75, the field, once again, protrudes at the top, but only by 9 x 13 cm. Columns 76 to 118 are again of the same length as columns 1 to 46.

div.head: State of Preservation
div.p: Generally good. The surface is quite even; the lower part is worn. A small horizontal crack extends ca. 87 cm above the floor from wall c into the first 3 columns. It continues, creating small crevices, through the entire wall. Another crack, ca. 110–117 cm above the floor, runs ca. 110 cm along the wall. A circular man-made hole cuts into columns 30–32, 191–195 cm above the floor.

row.cell: 大唐東京大敬愛寺一切經論目序釋靜泰撰; refers to the beginning of the Preface for the List of the Sutras and Treatises of the Great Jing’ai Monastery, in the Eastern Capital of the Great Tang, compiled by Monk Jingtai (Datang dongjing da Jing’ai si yiqie jinglun mu xu shi Jingtai zhuan 大唐東京大敬愛寺一切經論目序釋靜泰撰).
眾經目錄卷第一; refers to the beginning of scroll 1 of Jingtai’s Catalog of All Canonical Scriptures (Zhongjing mulu 眾經目錄).
眾經目錄卷第一; refers to the end of scroll 1 of Jingtai’s Catalog of All Canonical Scriptures.
眾經目錄卷第二; refers to the beginning of scroll 2 of Jingtai’s Catalog of All Canonical Scriptures.

……［辯中］邊［論二］卷, end of column; refers to the Defense of the Treatise on the Middle and the Extremes (Bianzhong bianlun 辯中邊論); the last engraved characters of Jingtai’s Catalog of All Canonical Scriptures.
摩訶般若波羅蜜 Great Perfection of Wisdom, in oversize characters (Mohe bore boluomi 摩訶般若波羅蜜).

大般涅槃經高貴德王菩薩品之二 十三; refers to the beginning of part two of the chapter entitled “Bodhisattva High and Noble Virtuous King” (Gaogui dewang pusa pin 高貴德王菩薩品), scroll 13 of the Great Parinirvāṇa Sutra (Da banniepan jing 大般涅槃經). The
Taishō
numbers this scroll as scroll 22.
大般涅槃經卷第十三; refers to the end of scroll 13 of the Nirvana Sutra, coinciding with the end of part 2 of the chapter entitled “Bodhisattva High and Noble Virtuous King”.

div.head: Layout

list.item: polished area: entire wall.

A field of script is present only in the eastern part; it forms a vertical rectangle, 270 x 174 cm; the text runs from left to right; columns are thus counted in this direction. No engraving was made on the western half of wall e.

div.p: In the upper half of the field of script in about a dozen areas, somewhat larger than the size of a palm, the characters have not been carved, although the sutra text could have continued across these empty areas.See fig. 12, p. 14, and discussion p. 33.

row.cell: vertical lines throughout, continuing for about 45 cm beyond column 77

figure.head: 46號窟內景；2010年拍攝
View into cave 46; photograph taken in 2010

div.head: State of Preservation
div.p: Generally good. The surface is rather even; the lower part is a slightly worn. Two man-made holes at a height of ca. 154 cm in the center of the wall; the eastern one between columns 63–65 does not affect the engraved text; the western one is outside the field of script. Another man-made hole, 237 cm above the floor affects column 77. A large natural hole, 45 cm wide, lies outside the field of script, 39–66 cm above the floor.

row.cell: ［佛性海藏智慧解脫破心相經（卷上） 如是我聞，一時佛……］; not carved; refers to the beginning of scroll 1 of the Sutra of Liberation and Breaking the Attributes of the Mind through the Wisdom Stored in the Ocean of Buddha-nature (Foxinghai zang zhihui jietuo po xinxiang jing 佛性海藏智慧解脫破心相經). There would have been enough available space for the two characters 卷上 in the title.
［二月］十五日臨般涅槃時; the first carved characters of scroll 1 of the Sutra on the Wisdom Stored in the Ocean of Buddha-nature.
佛性海藏智慧解脫破心相［經］卷上; refers to the end of scroll 1 of the Sutra on the Wisdom Stored in the Ocean of Buddha-nature.

list.item: Unlike on the opposite wall d, a pronounced triangular gable field is left with striations under the roof, and the polished area thus has a horizontal upper edge. The area begins 32 cm under the ridge and ends 22–36 cm above the floor; it extends to both walls e and g.

The field of script is nearly square, 253 x 228 cm; it begins ca. 12–27 cm from wall e, 37 cm below the ridge; it ends 54–56 cm from wall g and 30–37 cm above the floor. The upper edge is horizontal from columns 1–56; thereafter irregularly horizontal. The lower edge is approximately horizontal. This field of script contains four sutras:
columns 1–51, Diamond Sutra
columns 51–71, Sutra of the Buddha Crown
columns 71–84, Perfection of Wisdom in the Sutras
columns 84–98, Amitābha Sutra

row.cell: in the first 51 columns identical with grid, thereafter none
in the first 51 columns; ca. 2.5 x 2.5 cm, thereafter only vertical lines
in the first 51 columns, thereafter none

table.head: Sample, first text in grid (金剛)
Sample, second text (頂經), first column still in the grid

div.head: State of Preservation
div.p: Generally good; a horizontal crack 50 cm above the floor, starting from the corner with wall e, affects the first five columns. A string of natural crevices at the center of the wall, 59–68 cm above the floor affects columns 37–68; another shorter string 82–85 cm above the floor affects columns 51–67.

Several man-made incisions and holes. A vertical incision, 140 x 8 cm, has been cut into the wall 77 cm from wall e, affecting columns 19–24; a second vertical incision, 147 x 8 cm, 50 cm from wall g, affects columns 95–98; it continues in the floor, 52 cm into the cave, where it turns at a right angle and runs another 60 cm towards the entrance. These two incisions must have held a wooden fence or stone slabs, when, in the 1960s, the cave was used for keeping animals. Two holes have been hewn into the wall at a height of 193–196 cm (columns 36–37) and 192–195 cm (columns 58–60) above the floor.

row.cell: 金剛般若波羅蜜經; refers to the beginning of the Diamond Perfection of Wisdom Sutra (Jin’gang bore boluomi jing 金剛般若波羅蜜經).
金剛般若波羅蜜經; refers to the end of the Diamond Sutra.
佛頂尊勝陁羅尼經; refers to the beginning of the Sutra of the Glorious Buddha Crown Dhāraṇī (Foding zunsheng tuoluoni jing 佛頂尊勝陀羅尼經).
佛頂尊勝陁羅尼經; refers to the end of the Sutra of the Buddha Crown.
佛說脩多羅般若波羅蜜經; refers to the beginning of the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on the Perfection of Wisdom in the Sutras (Fo shuo Xiuduoluo bore boluomi jing 佛說脩多羅般若波羅蜜經).
脩多羅般若波羅蜜經; refers to the end of the Perfection of Wisdom in the Sutras.
佛說阿經; refers to the beginning of the Amitābha Sutra Spoken by the Buddha (Fo shuo Amituo jing 佛說阿彌陀經).
［……］難佛說［此經］……; the last visible characters of the Amitābha Sutra.

［……聞佛所說，歡喜信受，（作禮而去）］; destroyed; the last characters of the Amitābha Sutra; the characters 作禮而去 are recorded in the
Taishō
, but not in the Fangshan version (FS 0074), and not in all Dunhuang manuscripts.
［佛說阿弥陁經］; not visible; repetition of the title Amitābha Sutra by analogy with the other sutras on this wall.

div.head: Wall g
div.p: Polished area down to ca. 61–76 cm above the floor. No engraving.

list.item: The polished area is a vertical rectangle, 189 x 72 cm. It begins 18 cm under the lintel, and ends 42 cm above the floor, covering the entire width of the wall.
The field of script including the frame is a vertical rectangle, 157 x 72 cm. Some graffiti characters have been engraved on the polished area outside the field of script.

row.cell: double line frame; space between the lines at the sides 1.0 cm; in the lower frame ca. 2.0 cm;

div.head: State of Preservation
div.p: Smooth surface. A large triangular section in the upper left part has been cut off, taking away the first character in the first column of the inscription, and reaching down at the left to the midpoint of the wall. A vertical incision, 40 x 8 cm, was made below the field of script for a threshold. Two rectangular holes surrounded by chipped surfaces, 165–173 cm above the floor, affecting rows 7–10, and, 109–118 cm above the floor, affecting rows 13–17. A hole directly under the lintel, 14 cm from the corner with wall i.
More than 80% of the squares of the grid were originally not filled. Instead many characters poorly written and poorly engraved date from later periods.

row.cell: ［佛］頂尊勝陁羅尼咒; refers to the beginning of the Spell of the Glorious Buddha Crown Dhāraṇī (Foding zunsheng tuoluoni zhou 佛頂尊勝陀羅尼咒).
夏侯禪龕; later graffiti; refers to the meditation shrine of Duke Xia.
［莎（長引）婆］訶五十二 ; refers to the last, the fifty-second segment, of the Spell of the Buddha Crown .

大唐開元二十一［年□］月二十日; refers to the colophon with the date twenty-first year of the kaiyuan era. On the wall, the character er 二 has been changed into the character san
三 by adding a third stroke above (see fig. ).
如一; refers to Monk Ruyi 如一.
［阿弥阤佛說咒］; refers to the beginning of the Spell Spoken by Buddha Amitābha (Amituo fo shuo zhou 阿彌陀佛說咒).
［沙］門玄應; refers to Monk Xuanying.
